Analysing Government Disability Support Communications (2023–2026)

Tilly Thomas

Introduction

My results from beehive.govt.nz focused on all disability support releases from 2023-2026 under the National/ACT/New Zealand First Coalition Government.

Data sources:

Beehive New Zealand Government releases: https://www.beehive.govt.nz/

Wikipedia information: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page

Visualisation

The main purpose of my visualisation was to explore how different disability-support themes appeared in Beehive government releases between 2023–2026 and compare which ministers were most associated with different themes. I grouped release summaries into four main themes: support services, funding, healthcare and wellbeing, and housing and community support. The visualisation shows the number of releases from each minister that matched these themes in their summary. Each ministers name is coloured by the colour of the party they belong to.

## Appendix

scrape_search_results <- function(page_path) {
  # Read HTML
  html_doc <- read_html(page_path)
  
  # Extract the isolated search result nodes (the individual cards)
  # This will depend on how you've acquired the results
  # Look for .search-result or .teaser (though there may be others)
  nodes <- html_doc %>% html_elements(".search-result")
  
  # Map over each individual search result
  map_df(nodes, function(node) {
    # Title
    title_element <- node %>% html_element("h3 a")
    title <- title_element %>% html_text2()
    
    # Date and time
    date_element <- node %>% html_element("time")
    datetime  <- date_element %>% html_attr("datetime") # Raw timestamp
    date_text <- date_element %>% html_text2()          # Human-readable text
    
    # Summary
    summary <- node %>% html_element(".views-field-search-api-excerpt") %>% html_text2()
    
    # Ministers
    minister <- node %>%
      html_elements(".teaser--small") %>%
      html_text2() %>%
      # trim out excess white space noise, and collapse them using a semicolon
      str_trim() %>%
      paste(collapse = ";")
    
    # Portfolios
    portfolio <- node %>%
      html_elements(".taxonomy-term--type-portfolios") %>%
      html_text2() %>%
      # trim out excess white space noise, and collapse them using a semicolon
      str_trim() %>%
      paste(collapse = ";")
    
    # Combine individual results into a tidy tibble
    tibble(
      datetime   = datetime,
      date_text  = date_text,
      title      = title,
      ministers  = minister,
      portfolios = portfolio,
      summary    = summary
    )
  })
}
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(rvest)

file_names <- list.files("html") %>%
  paste0("html/", .)

source("scrape_html.R")

# use map_df with file_names and read_csv
beehive <- map_df(file_names, scrape_search_results)

beehive %>%
  duplicated() %>%
  sum()

saveRDS(beehive, "beehive.rds")

minister_names <- beehive %>%
  select(ministers) %>%
  separate_rows(ministers, sep = ";") %>%
  mutate(ministers = str_to_lower(ministers)) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  pull(ministers)
 
minister_names

source("get_wikipedia_infobox.R")                            

ministers <- map_df(minister_names, get_wikipedia_infobox)

ministers <- ministers %>%
  distinct()

ministers %>%
  duplicated() %>%
  sum()

saveRDS(ministers,"ministers.rds")
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(rvest)

#Reading in data 
beehive_data<-readRDS("beehive.rds")
ministers_data<-readRDS("ministers.rds")

#extracting parties 
extract<- ministers_data%>%
  filter(str_detect(label, regex("party", ignore_case = TRUE)))

#Editing minister info, grouping role by minister
minister_only <- ministers_data %>%
  mutate(minister = str_to_lower(minister)) %>%
  group_by(minister)%>%
  summarise(role = first(role))

#joining minister info together
minister_info<-inner_join(minister_only, extract, by= ("minister"))

#Ensuring each row is one minister
beehive_info <- beehive_data %>%
  separate_rows(ministers, sep = ";") %>%
  mutate(ministers = str_to_lower(ministers)) 
  
#Combining the two data sets together  
combined_data <- left_join(beehive_info, minister_info, by = c("ministers"= "minister"))

#Mutating data to pull specific concepts from the summaries
support_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(
      str_to_lower(summary),
      "support|supports|services|service|care|carers|respite"))%>%
  mutate(theme = "Support Services")

funding_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(
      str_to_lower(summary),
      "funding|funded|investment|budget|financial|cost|costs")) %>%
  mutate(theme = "Funding")

healthcare_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(
      str_to_lower(summary),
      "health|mental health|wellbeing|well-being"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(theme = "Healthcare & Wellbeing")

housing_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(
      str_to_lower(summary),
      "housing|community|volunteer"
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(theme = "Housing & Community")

##Binding all rows together for theme and mutating data so all ministers names start with capital letters
plot_data <- bind_rows(
  support_data,
  funding_data,
  healthcare_data,
  housing_data)%>%
mutate(ministers = str_replace_all(ministers, "\\S+", function(x) paste0(str_to_upper(str_sub(x, 1, 1)), str_sub(x, 2))))

#Picking colors
my_colours <- c("#CF7280","#EEDF77","#7AC1E0","#3AAF7D","#E9E9E9","#585858")

#Making theme
my_theme <- theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = my_colours[5]),
                  panel.background = element_rect(fill = my_colours[5]),
                  legend.key = element_rect(fill = my_colours[5]),
                  legend.background = element_rect(fill = my_colours[5]),
                  panel.grid = element_line(color = "#585858"),
                  axis.ticks = element_blank(),
                  axis.line = element_blank(),
                  plot.margin = margin(1,1,1,1,"cm"),
                  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
                  plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

#Comparison graph of minister by number of releases filled with themes 
plot_data2 <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    party_colour = case_when(
      str_detect(value, ("ACT")) ~ "#d4a90f",
      str_detect(value, ("NZ First|New Zealand First")) ~ "#000000",
      str_detect(value, ("Independent")) ~ "#585858",
      str_detect(value, ("National")) ~ "#00529F",
      TRUE ~ "grey40"
    )
  )

axis_colours <- plot_data2 %>%
  select(ministers, party_colour) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  arrange(ministers) %>%
  pull(party_colour)

plot_data2 %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(aes(
    y = ministers,
    fill = theme
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Disability-support themes discussed by ministers (2023-2026)",
    subtitle = "Comparing support-service, funding, healthcare, and housing themes",
    caption = "Source: Beehive NZ and Wikipedia",
    y = "Minister",
    x = "Number of releases"
  ) +
  my_theme +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colours)+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(colour = axis_colours))


#Saving visual graph
ggsave("my_viz.png")
